name: Lu's Spinach-Stuffed Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
2. Pierce each potato in two or three places.
3. Rub potatoes lightly with olive oil. In a microwave oven, arrange the potatoes in a circle with the thickest parts to the outside and cook on high power for 10 minutes, turning them every 3 minutes until they begin to whistle.
4. Bake in the oven for 15-20 minutes until they can be easily pierced with a fork (Microwave method saves time; alternatively, bake the potatoes in a 375 degrees F oven for 45-60 minutes).
5. While the potatoes are baking, sauté the onion in a few drops of olive oil until soft. Add the spinach and sauté about 30 seconds until soft.
6. Remove from heat and mix in a bowl with the salt, pepper, and evaporated milk (If using frozen, simply stir into onions and remove from heat).
7. When the potatoes are done, cut them in half lengthwise and scoop out the flesh with a spoon.
8. Add to the spinach/evening milk mixture and mash together with a fork.
9. Arrange the skins on a cookie sheet and spoon the mixture into the skins. Sprinkle with the grated cheese and cayenne pepper.
10. Return to oven at 375 degrees F for about 20 minutes.
Tried 08/28/98: Served with meatloaf. Season with salt after adding the parmesan, which is quite salty. Used lots of pepper.
Adapted from Mother Earth News recipe.
From the recipe files of RecipeLu
